common.confirmLogout: Are you sure you want to log out?
common.routeHint: This is a scaffold view, Real API wiring comes next

auth.wallBody: From draft to artifacts, the flow stays clear and resumable
auth.panelBody: Keep recovery, feedback, and reviewable artifacts visible

login.title: Continue your config workspace
login.body: Resume drafts, review output, and generate configs in one place
login.helper: Use your account to continue current work

register.title: Create a local account
register.body: A new account manages its own drafts and artifacts, and the admin account stays system-owned
register.helper: Use a stable email so the workspace stays easy to identify

wizard.title: Fill the real deployment config in stages, then review and generate once
wizard.body: The flow now covers frontend and backend routing, nodes, proxy modes, rate limits, and rewrite testing instead of only collecting a few entry values
wizard.deferDraftCreation: This draft stays local for now, It will be created on the server when you generate the final config
wizard.nameHint: This is only for your own list view
wizard.embyUrlHint: Use the real Emby base URL without the trailing slash
wizard.embyPortHint: Usually 8096 unless your instance uses another port
wizard.embyTokenHint: The frontend proxy uses this to access Emby APIs safely
wizard.logRootHint: Start here when something breaks, The admin log page reads runtime logs from here too
wizard.logLevelHint: Use debug while validating, Keep info for normal production runs
wizard.logPrefixHint: Useful when several instances share the same log directory
wizard.memoryModeHint: Low saves memory, Middle stays balanced, High leans toward performance
wizard.encipherKeyHint: Do not ship the template default, Replace it before production
wizard.encipherIvHint: Rotate this together with the signature key
wizard.frontendPortHint: Users hit this first, It proxies Emby and rewrites the entry flow
wizard.backendPortHint: Real media leaves from here, In dual mode it must differ from the frontend port
wizard.backendBaseUrlHint: Use the final public base URL clients should see, not the upstream node address
wizard.backendPublicPortHint: Fill this when the public entry is not 80 or 443
wizard.backendPathHint: The path segment clients should reach for streaming, Leave blank for root
wizard.problematicClientsHint: One keyword per line, These clients skip some aggressive optimizations
wizard.userAgentModeHint: Pick allow-list or deny-list first, then fill the matching rules
wizard.fallbackPathHint: Optional, Use this when the requested file is missing
wizard.sslCertHint: Only useful for backend HTTPS, Leave blank to keep default discovery
wizard.sslKeyHint: Pair this with the certificate path
wizard.deployNginxBody: These fields control the generated nginx.conf domain, TLS paths, and DNS resolver, without changing the app runtime HTTP2 config
wizard.nginxFrontendServerNameHint: Domain written into nginx.conf for frontend or dual-mode output
wizard.nginxFrontendSslCertHint: Leave blank to emit only a reminder comment, then fill the real path yourself
wizard.nginxFrontendSslKeyHint: Leave blank to emit only a reminder comment, then pair it with the certificate path
wizard.nginxBackendServerNameHint: Domain written into nginx.conf for the backend streaming entry
wizard.nginxBackendSslCertHint: Leave blank to emit only a reminder comment, then fill the real path yourself
wizard.nginxBackendSslKeyHint: Leave blank to emit only a reminder comment, then pair it with the certificate path
wizard.nginxResolverProviderHint: Resolver is only needed when backend Nginx proxies upstream HTTPS. No provider is preselected by default
wizard.nginxCustomResolversHint: Use nginx resolver syntax and separate multiple addresses with spaces
wizard.deployRuntimeBody: These fields control generated systemd.service and pm2.config.cjs paths so the defaults do not carry unrelated project names
wizard.systemdBinaryPathHint: Default is /usr/bin/embystream. If yours is different, run which embystream first and paste the real path here
wizard.systemdWorkingDirectoryHint: Defaults to /opt/stream. Change it to match your deployment layout
wizard.systemdConfigPathHint: Defaults to /opt/stream/config.toml. Keep it aligned with your real mounted config
wizard.pm2BinaryPathHint: Default is /usr/bin/embystream. If yours is different, run which embystream first and paste the real path here
wizard.pm2WorkingDirectoryHint: Defaults to /opt/stream, /opt/stream-frontend, or /opt/stream-backend based on the selected mode
wizard.pm2ConfigPathHint: Defaults to config.toml inside the working directory, but you can point it elsewhere
wizard.pm2OutFileHint: Defaults to logs/pm2.out.log inside the working directory
wizard.pm2ErrorFileHint: Defaults to logs/pm2.err.log inside the working directory
wizard.frontendAntiReverseHint: Enable host validation when the frontend entry is exposed to the internet
wizard.frontendAntiHostHint: Use host or host:port only, do not include the scheme; separate multiple domains with commas
wizard.frontendRewriteBody: These rules rewrite the public entry path before forwarding traffic to Emby or the backend
wizard.rewritePatternHint: Start from a concrete path sample and tighten only when needed
wizard.rewriteReplacementHint: Capture groups such as $1 are supported
wizard.rewriteRegexNoMatch: The current sample did not match
wizard.noRewriteRules: No rewrite rules are configured yet
wizard.routeTestHint: Paste a real media path here and check the rewrite result before generating
wizard.routeTestNoMatch: No enabled rewrite rule matched this sample
wizard.routeChecklistItem1: Validate a real sample path here before you save the draft
wizard.routeChecklistItem2: After generation, inspect Frontend.PathRewrite and BackendNode.PathRewrite in config.toml directly
wizard.routeChecklistItem3: Before production, hit one real media link from a client and confirm routing, node selection, and delivery mode
wizard.nodeFlowBody: Every backend node needs its own match scope, proxy strategy, rate limit, and rewrite rules, The backend should not stop at a single listener port
wizard.nodeNameHint: Mainly for config readability and troubleshooting
wizard.nodePatternHint: Usually the decrypted file path prefix or regex
wizard.nodeBaseUrlHint: This is the real upstream target: NAS, OpenList, WebDAV, and so on
wizard.nodePortHint: Fill this when the upstream port is not the default one
wizard.nodePathHint: Use this when the upstream service does not live at root
wizard.nodePriorityHint: Lower numbers win first, Set this explicitly when several nodes can match
wizard.nodeProxyModeHint: redirect, proxy, and accel_redirect change both security and bandwidth behavior
wizard.nodeSpeedLimitHint: 0 means unlimited, The limiter applies per device
wizard.nodeBurstLimitHint: Give players some short burst budget so startup does not stutter immediately
wizard.nodeAntiReverseHint: Only enable this when a node needs its own host validation
wizard.nodeAntiHostHint: Leave blank unless the node has a dedicated public host; separate multiple domains with commas
wizard.nodeRouteTestHint: This test shows the rewrite result after traffic has already matched the node
wizard.backendNodeRequired: Add at least one backend node before moving to the next step
wizard.diskDescriptionHint: Describe the mount point or disk purpose for future maintenance
wizard.openListPortHint: Leave blank if the port already lives inside the URL
wizard.openListTokenHint: Used when the node calls the OpenList API directly
wizard.directLinkUserAgentHint: Some upstream CDNs only accept a fixed User-Agent
wizard.googleNodeUuidHint: Token cache and refresh state depend on this stable ID
wizard.googleClientIdHint: The OAuth client ID for the desktop or web app you created
wizard.googleClientSecretHint: Required together with the client ID to refresh tokens
wizard.googleDriveIdHint: Preferred over drive_name when both exist
wizard.googleDriveNameHint: Used as the fallback lookup key when drive_id is absent
wizard.googleAccessTokenHint: Can stay blank at first if refresh_token is available
wizard.googleRefreshTokenHint: This is the long-lived credential the node really needs
wizard.webdavNodeUuidHint: Must stay stable and unique when accel_redirect is used
wizard.webdavUrlModeHint: Controls how the final file path is assembled for upstream requests
wizard.webdavQueryParamHint: Used only when url_mode is query_path
wizard.webdavUrlTemplateHint: Required only for url_template mode and must include {file_path}
wizard.webdavUsernameHint: Fill this when the upstream expects Basic Auth
wizard.webdavUserAgentHint: Some WebDAV servers restrict the accepted user agent
wizard.reviewBody: This is the last technical pass over ports, nodes, rewrites, and testing steps before generation
wizard.reviewChecklistItem1: Confirm the public ports, base URLs, and path segments match the target environment
wizard.reviewChecklistItem2: Confirm every backend node has its own match rule, delivery mode, and required credentials
wizard.reviewChecklistItem3: Confirm every enabled rewrite rule has been validated with a sample path
wizard.reviewChecklistItem4: Confirm the rate limits, host guards, and fallback behavior match production expectations
wizard.helperLabel: How this answer changes the generated result
wizard.modeFrontendBody: Use this when you only need a proxy entry in front of Emby
wizard.modeBackendBody: Use this when the media edge should run on a separate host
wizard.modeDualBody: Use this when you want one service to cover both sides first

stepCards.title: Start with the running shape
stepCards.purpose: Pick whether you want only the proxy, only the media edge, or both sides together
stepCards.effect: This choice controls which ports and deployment files matter later

stepCards.title: Fill the shared runtime basics first
stepCards.purpose: Name the draft, set logging, choose the cache profile, and rotate the signing secrets
stepCards.effect: This stage affects troubleshooting, runtime behavior, and whether you accidentally ship template defaults

stepCards.title: Wire the real entry and public backend address
stepCards.purpose: This stage covers the real Emby target, the frontend listener, the backend public address, and problematic clients
stepCards.effect: These values control how clients enter the system and what the generated deployment artifacts expose

stepCards.title: Apply guardrails and fallback behavior
stepCards.purpose: Control UA policy, host validation, TLS paths, and the fallback media response
stepCards.effect: This changes abuse resistance, HTTPS wiring, and what users see when playback fails

stepCards.title: Validate rewrite logic before generation
stepCards.purpose: Do not leave rewrites as untested config text, Run a sample path through the rules first
stepCards.effect: You can catch bad regexes, wrong replacements, and broken public path assumptions before deployment

stepCards.title: Create and finish every backend node
stepCards.purpose: Each node should own its own match rule, delivery mode, rate limit, rewrites, and upstream-specific credentials
stepCards.effect: The backend becomes deployable and debuggable instead of stopping at a single listener section

stepCards.title: Review once before generation
stepCards.purpose: Run through the key deployment values, node count, and test actions one more time
stepCards.effect: The generated snapshot and artifacts are created from this exact version

wizard.noBackendNodesNeeded: This mode does not start a backend service, so this stage can be skipped
wizard.previewBody: Every file can be expanded, collapsed, and downloaded on its own

drafts.title: Drafts and progress stay here
drafts.body: Resume, rename, or clear drafts without starting over
drafts.queueBody: Drafts stay editable and generated results move into the config library
drafts.loadingBody: Drafts and config-set counts load before the workspace appears
drafts.emptyBody: Start a new draft and come back when you need it

account.title: Account and shortcuts
account.body: See the current account, role, and common entry points
account.roleAdminBody: Includes logs and the full workspace, and final access is still enforced by the API
account.roleUserBody: Scoped to personal drafts and generated artifacts
account.aboutBody: Open the project page, check the current version, and read release notes
account.fontsTitle: Choose the Chinese and Latin fonts you actually want to read
account.fontsBody: Chinese and English preferences are saved locally and applied to the full workspace immediately
account.fontPreviewZh: Use this line to judge rhythm, density, and the overall feel of Chinese copy
account.fontPreviewEn: Preview the Latin font pairing across labels, headings, and mixed-language content
account.quickLinksBody: Keep frequent pages close and navigation quiet
account.securityBody: The client guides the flow and access control is still enforced by the API
account.passwordCardBody: Regular users can only change their own login password here, and a successful change signs out the current session and requires a fresh login
account.passwordDialogBody: Enter your current password and set a new login password, and the two new-password entries must match exactly
account.passwordRepeatMismatch: The two new passwords do not match

settings.title: Workspace settings
settings.body: Manage interface language and typography in one place
settings.themeBody: Lock the UI to light or dark, or follow the phone or desktop system theme automatically
settings.languageBody: Updates apply immediately to copy and time formatting
settings.registrationBody: Control whether visitors can create local accounts from the login screen

dashboard.title: Runtime overview
dashboard.body: Monitor disk, CPU, memory, and uptime
dashboard.refreshHint: These metrics are derived from the EmbyStream main process and related files, not the full machine state
dashboard.loadingBody: Metrics will appear here once they are ready

more.title: More
more.body: Browse additional entry points by function group, and move into each page like a native app

docs.title: Docs Wiki
docs.body: Browse runtime docs and Web admin deployment notes in one place
docs.readmeBody: Read the main runtime guide and parameter reference
docs.nginxBody: Use a reverse proxy to forward your domain to the Web admin page, then access EmbyStream through that domain
docs.issueLink: Open an issue for doc feedback

configSets.title: Configs
configSets.body: Review, duplicate, and download generated configs
configSets.libraryBody: Browse configs as a clean list, then open details only when you need the generated files
configSets.detailBody: Browse generated artifacts for this config and expand each file only when needed
configSets.loadingBody: The latest results appear as a collapsible list once loading finishes
configSets.detailLoadingBody: Artifacts will appear here once this config is ready
configSets.detailMissingBody: The requested config could not be found or loaded
configSets.errorTitle: The config library is unavailable right now
configSets.emptyBody: Generate a config first, then inspect and download it here

logs.title: Runtime logs are admin-only
logs.body: The client shows access state clearly and the server remains the control point
logs.consoleTitle: View recent logs in one place
logs.consoleBody: Filter recent records by source, level, and keyword to find issues faster
logs.filteredTitle: No logs match the current filters
logs.filteredBody: Try a broader level or keyword
logs.securityNote: Frontend guards improve flow, but the real boundary stays in the API
logs.emptyBody: Runtime, stream, and audit records will appear here once the service starts writing logs
logs.filterAllHint: Shows stream logs, Web admin runtime logs, and audit records together for a quick recent overview.
logs.filterStreamHint: Covers EmbyStream runtime activity such as gateway, proxy, forwarding, and streaming pipeline behavior.
logs.filterRuntimeHint: Covers the Web Studio itself, including bootstrap, listening, initialization, and API runtime state rather than stream traffic.
logs.filterAuditHint: Covers sign-in, user management, and config changes so you can see who changed what and when.
logs.statusReconnectingBody: Trying to recover the live log stream.
logs.statusOfflineBody: Live updates are paused until the page reconnects.
logs.followLiveBody: The console stays pinned to the newest log entries.
logs.followPausedBody: Scroll back to the top or resume to follow new entries again.
logs.followPausedCount: {count} new log entries are waiting above.

users.title: User management
users.body: Manage registered users, roles, status, and passwords
users.filterTitle: Find users by username or email
users.loadingBody: Users will appear here once the list is ready
users.emptyTitle: No users match the current filter
users.emptyBody: Adjust the keyword and try again
users.passwordPrompt: Enter a new password with at least 8 characters
users.passwordDialogBody: Set a new password for {username}, and the user will need to sign in again

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.body: The web style is inspired by Claude Code, and the code was written with OpenAI Codex. If this causes an issue, please file a GitHub issue
